buying_signals.text: Supabase is actively targeting larger customers with an enterprise-focused pricing tier that offers custom solutions, Uptime SLAs, and BYO Cloud support, alongside a 'Team' plan starting at $599/month which includes compliance features like SOC2 and HIPAA.

buying_signals.text: The company showcases strong customer success stories focused on scalability and cost savings, such as an 83% reduction in data infrastructure costs for one client and another scaling to one million users in seven months, demonstrating value for growing businesses.

Berri AI boosts productivity by migrating from AWS RDS to Supabase Vector
Firecrawl switches from Pinecone to Supabase Vector for PostgreSQL vector embeddings.

pricing_trend.text: The pricing structure includes usage-based billing for compute resources, with paid plans receiving credits to offset costs, and a default spend cap on the Pro plan to prevent unexpected charges.

citations.snippet: Paid Plans come with $10 /month in compute credits to cover one Micro instance or offset the cost of any other instance.
The Pro Plan has a spend cap enabled by default to keep costs under control. If you want to scale beyond the plan's included quota, simply switch off the spend cap to pay for additional resources.

executive_summary: Supabase's aggressive move upmarket, coupled with a nascent GTM team and tiered security model, creates an immediate opening to position our platform as essential for de-risking their enterprise growth. — act this quarter.

strategic_plan.headline: Supabase's aggressive move upmarket, coupled with a nascent GTM team and tiered security model, creates an immediate opening to position our platform as essential for de-risking their enterprise growth.
strategic_plan.narrative:
Supabase, an open-source developer platform, is rapidly commercializing its massive community of 500k+ developers by launching a formal enterprise sales motion. They are moving upmarket with new pricing tiers, acquiring companies like Hydra to accelerate their roadmap, and explicitly targeting competitors' customers with claims of better cost and performance. This transition from a product-led growth (PLG) company to a hybrid model with a dedicated sales force represents a critical inflection point.

The urgency to engage is driven by their active, real-time build-out of a global go-to-market function. Current job openings for Enterprise AEs, Solutions Architects, a 'GTM Engineer,' and partner managers signal they are constructing the sales engine and its supporting operational and technical stack right now. Engaging them during this foundational phase provides a unique opportunity to become an embedded part of their growth infrastructure, rather than attempting to displace an established solution later.
The primary risk for Supabase—and opportunity for us—lies in the dissonance between their enterprise ambitions and their current offering's maturity. Significant security and compliance gaps exist in their lower tiers, such as HIPAA as a paid add-on, severely limited audit logs on the free plan, and lack of automatic backups. As their new sales team targets larger, more discerning customers, these gaps will become significant friction points in their sales cycle. Furthermore, their reliance on third-party integrations and an open-source model expands their supply chain risk, a key concern for the enterprise clients they now seek.
strategic_plan.icp_rationale: Supabase is a fast-growing tech company moving into the enterprise segment and actively hiring a global GTM team, indicating a need for scalable sales, compliance, and security solutions.
strategic_plan.deal_size_rationale: This estimate is based on their investment in a global enterprise GTM team, their need to equip that team to win seven-figure deals, and the requirement to solve compliance and security gaps (e.g., SOC2, HIPAA) to be credible in the enterprise market.
strategic_plan.urgency_rationale: Active hiring for a global GTM team and a 'GTM Engineer' indicates they are building their sales engine now, creating a critical window to influence their tech stack and processes.

recommended_plays.text: GTM team to initiate multi-threaded outreach to new sales leadership and the 'GTM Engineer' role, positioning our solution as critical infrastructure to build a scalable and efficient enterprise sales motion.
recommended_plays.rationale: Supabase is building its GTM function from the ground up and will need tooling to support scalable growth; engaging the roles responsible for building these systems is the most direct path to embedding our solution.

citations.snippet: Supabase is significantly expanding its go-to-market function by hiring for numerous sales and customer-facing roles globally, including Enterprise Account Executives, Solutions Architects, and regional sales leads for APAC and LATAM.
The company is investing in revenue operations by hiring a "GTM Engineer", indicating a focus on building scalable systems and processes to support its sales and marketing growth.

recommended_plays.text: Security and GTM teams to co-develop a 'Compliance Upsell' motion, highlighting how our platform helps bridge the security gaps (e.g., limited logs, add-on HIPAA) between Supabase's lower tiers and the expectations of their new enterprise targets.
recommended_plays.rationale: Supabase's own pricing model creates a security and compliance gap that their new enterprise sales team will need to solve for larger customers. We can be the solution that enables them to sell upmarket effectively.

citations.snippet: HIPAA compliance is not included by default and is only available as a paid add-on for the 'Team' plan, posing a risk for healthcare applications built on lower tiers.
The free tier offers only one hour of authentication audit logs, severely limiting the ability to investigate security incidents.

recommended_plays.text: GTM to create a targeted campaign for Supabase's sales team, showcasing how we help them win more AI-focused customers by addressing the underlying third-party and supply chain risks inherent in their AI integrations.
recommended_plays.rationale: Supabase is successfully attracting AI companies, which are highly sensitive to security and third-party risk. We can position our solution as a sales accelerant for them in this key growth market.

citations.snippet: The platform is gaining strong traction within the AI development space, with customers like Berri AI, Humata, and Markprompt using it for AI-powered applications.
Supabase has deep integrations with and dependencies on external services like OpenAI, Hugging Face, and Stripe, exposing it to potential risks from those third parties.

recommended_plays.text: GTM leadership to engage Supabase's new partner-focused hires to explore a joint strategy targeting the AWS ecosystem, positioning our platform as a value-add for their cloud marketplace and co-sell motions.
recommended_plays.rationale: They are explicitly hiring to build out their partner ecosystem, particularly around AWS. This provides a formal channel to establish a strategic alliance early in their partner program's development.
